metalmind_title: Big Bend Burro – Final Technical Blueprint

source_registry.path: 03-08 Conversation_ AI-Free Days, Big Bend Activism, Health Wins, and Tourism Startup Plans-Summary.txt
03-08 Conversation_ AI-Free Days, Big Bend Activism, Health Wins, and Tourism Startup Plans-transcript.txt

program_principles: Build the full system architecture now, but activate customer-facing and operator-facing features gradually.
Protect the private retirement/home zone from the public tourism zone.
Solve water, wastewater, power, and access before scaling lodging.
Prefer premium, differentiated experiences over commoditized capacity.
Use local labor, local guides, and local makers wherever feasible.
Treat activism as conservation-aligned business defense, not as a separate gimmick.
Avoid AI Hub; use pragmatic SaaS and custom AI only where it materially helps.
Design all customer systems to degrade gracefully in low-connectivity conditions.

business_context.primary_goal: Create a sustainable Big Bend eco-tourism and artisan project that can start small, teach Chuck and Susan gradually, and expand into a durable local business with conservation value.
secondary_goals: Support construction and planning of the retirement cabin/home site.
Create additional revenue from workshops, equipment rental, artisan products, and photography.
Build a digital presence that can drive tourism, community awareness, and conservation support.
Create a scoped AI assistant that helps operators and later helps guests.
non_goals: Do not build a general-purpose AI Hub.
Do not launch a large multi-machine rental yard in phase 0 or phase 1.
Do not launch regulated ferry/cross-border transport as an initial product.
Do not rely on summer daytime occupancy for business viability.
Do not depend on fragile online tile shipping as the core artisan revenue stream.

facts.notes: Spring is primary peak; June-August are structurally weak; fall-winter is strong shoulder/secondary peak.

facts.workshop_advantage: Experience-led sales are more viable than pure heavy-item ecommerce because shipping is expensive and fragile.

facts.business_rule: Workshops are the cash engine; prints are the margin layer; stock is long-tail; merch is a support SKU set.

activation_profiles.description: Feature is built but hidden; operator only watches reports or internal demos.
Feature is visible and usable with guided steps, presets, and human approval gates.
Feature is in regular use with SOPs and guardrails, but still has audit logging and rollback.
Feature is fully trusted by operators; advanced settings and automation are available.

operator_onboarding_model.global_rule: Default all new features to R0_OBSERVE or R1_ASSISTED. Promotion to a higher readiness profile requires successful use in the prior profile and explicit operator approval.

directories.purpose: raw transcripts, notes, photos, external evidence
canonical packs for website and assistant
assistant policies, tool allowlists, feature flags
structured content, MDX, and media manifests
policy gateway rules, acl maps, retention
infrastructure as code, compose files, env templates
lookup tables, default content, design tokens

outputs: capsule with DARQ register and anchor map

design_rules: Use dark-sky-safe visual language: no harsh neon color schemes, avoid large bright white nighttime hero sections in public-facing dark mode.
Use Susan's photos as the core visual identity for hero sections, workshop promotions, artisan pages, and blog covers.
Separate public tourism imagery from private home/cabin imagery unless explicitly approved for sharing.
Preserve image copyright metadata and maintain media manifests for each photo.

storage: S3-compatible object storage or Cloudflare R2

routes.purpose: craft, photography, retreat, and special event booking
equipment rental catalog and booking rules
artisan tiles, pottery, prints, small merch
border wall updates, scripts, donations, local coalition links
Chuck and Susan story, mission, stewardship
updates, field notes, construction journal, workshop recaps
steel building consultation service for ranches and businesses

feature_flags.description: All checkout flows display Coming Soon badges until payment integration activated
PayPal, Venmo, Zelle, Credit Card UI stubs (visual only, non-functional)

architecture.core_runtime: API-based LLM plus deterministic orchestration layer

internal_modules.purpose: Help Chuck with cabin design, property tasks, material lists, and phased land-use planning.
Help Susan with tile collection planning, workshop prep, pricing, photography product selection, and shipping rules.
Answer guest questions about stays, routes, weather, stargazing, workshops, and etiquette.
Assemble weekend-cruise-style packages from lodging plus excursions.
Surface current border-wall status, local resolutions, contact scripts, and donation actions.
Draft follow-up emails, reminders, post-stay requests, and workshop upsells.

hard_rules: Always cite canonical packs internally before producing a factual answer.
Never invent availability, pricing, legal advice, or safety guidance.
When uncertainty exists, return unknown_or_needs_review rather than hallucinating.
Do not access or reveal private home-zone details unless explicitly tagged public.
Treat activism content as factual and evidence-grounded.

integrations.purpose: Rental catalog, quotes, calendar, damage deposit handling
Low-code orchestration between booking, email, crm, analytics and assistant
Seasonal updates, workshop announcements, activism news

integrations.note: Prefer provider abstraction layer to avoid lock-in.

zones.description: retirement home and personal use area
night sky decks, scenic observation areas
offsite or right-of-way excursions, river access, ghost-town routes

separation_rules: No public booking should place guests in PRIVATE_HOME_ZONE.
Workshop activities can overlap with PUBLIC_STAY_ZONE only when scheduled and staffed.
Equipment operations must remain outside guest movement paths during stays and events.
Public-facing maps should reveal only approved access routes and meeting points.

feature_registry.name: weekend_cruise_itinerary_builder
feature_registry.description: Build curated weekend packages where guests select lodging plus river, star, craft, or history excursions.

feature_registry.name: artisan_tile_collections
feature_registry.description: Sell limited handmade tile runs, decorative pieces, and workshop follow-up orders with local pickup and cautious shipping.

feature_registry.name: photography_workshops
feature_registry.description: Offer dark-sky and landscape workshops with small cohorts and premium pricing.

feature_registry.name: craft_workshops
feature_registry.description: Offer table-format tile-making and hand-building workshops tied to the region and Susan's style.

feature_registry.name: equipment_rental_service
feature_registry.description: Local delivery-based mini-excavator or skid-steer rental program with deposits and inspection logs.

feature_registry.name: cabin_and_land_planning_assistant
feature_registry.description: Internal-only assistant module for Chuck's cabin build, deck/railing/bathroom priorities, and long-term land use.

feature_registry.name: activism_hub
feature_registry.description: Public education and action center around the Big Bend border-wall controversy.

feature_registry.name: livestream_and_webcam_network
feature_registry.description: Scenic and night-sky cameras for marketing, environmental witness, and guest engagement.

feature_registry.name: mining_history_experience
feature_registry.description: Surface-only interpretive mining experience with simulated panning and strict safety limits; no dangerous abandoned-mine entry.

feature_registry.name: river_and_boquillas_partner_packages
feature_registry.description: Package lodging with partner-led river trips and Boquillas day experiences rather than owning regulated transport early.

feature_registry.name: retreat_hosting
feature_registry.description: Support photography retreats, writing retreats, wellness weekends, and private group bookings.

feature_registry.name: local_marketplace
feature_registry.description: Catalog and eventually sell local maker goods or partner experiences without overcommitting to fulfillment complexity.

feature_registry.name: property_and_probate_tracker
feature_registry.description: Internal issue tracker for land title, probate, wrong-tax parcel, adjoining-lot acquisition, and permit dependencies.

feature_registry.name: bilingual_support
feature_registry.description: Spanish-language support for selected website routes, assistant flows, and workshop materials.

feature_registry.name: steel_buildings_service
feature_registry.description: Consultation and brokering service for commercial steel buildings. Chuck helps local ranchers and businesses source pre-engineered metal buildings.

phases.name: foundation
phases.objective: Create the machine, memory, and site foundations without overwhelming Chuck and Susan.
build_scope: internal Burro modules for property and artisan planning
activation_scope: private prototype website or staging site
first unit feasibility and budget tracking

gates.requirement: all source files ingested and registered
l2 ds/dr packs and conversation capsule created
high-risk factual claims verified in l3 packs
property title/probate blockers logged with next actions
water/waste/power options priced for one-unit pilot

acceptance_tests: Operator can view a single dashboard of property tasks, research packs, and next actions.
Design tokens and media manifests exist for all approved photos.
A single-unit pilot budget and payback model are stored in machine-readable form.

phases.name: mvp_launch
phases.objective: Open a small but polished public presence and test real demand with manageable operational scope.
activation_scope: 1-4 lodging units depending capital and readiness

gates.requirement: public site passes accessibility and payment smoke tests
guest communications and waiver flows tested
shipping rules enforced for fragile products
Burro guest answers cite only approved memory views

acceptance_tests: A guest can book a stay, book a workshop, and receive a confirmation email.
An operator can publish or unpublish a workshop without developer help.
Burro can answer 20 golden FAQs with source-backed responses and no unsafe claims.
At least one seasonal pricing ruleset can be changed without code deployment.

phases.name: expansion
phases.objective: Diversify revenue, activate experience bundles, and strengthen Burro as an orchestrator while keeping human approval in the loop.

gates.requirement: inspection, deposit, and tax logic tested
camera feeds stable and low-light compliant
operators comfortable at R2 on core workflows

acceptance_tests: Burro can assemble a multi-step itinerary draft and present it for review.
Rental bookings can be created, rescheduled, and closed with inspection evidence.
Livestream and webcam pages work under constrained bandwidth conditions.

phases.name: mature_operation
phases.objective: Run a stable, locally integrated tourism and artisan business with strong conservation identity and measured automation.

gates.requirement: Metalmind maturity level M4 for production knowledge
regular eval suite and regression controls live
partnership governance and revenue share policies approved

acceptance_tests: Core revenue streams are tracked monthly and compared to plan.
Public-facing content can be updated by operators without breaking policy rules.
All customer-critical workflows have incident runbooks and rollback paths.

first_90_day_backlog.title: Create conversation capsule and DARQ register from transcript and summary
Publish L3 packs for glamping economics, equipment rental, tourism, border wall, handicrafts, photography
Create property/probate tracker and adjacent-lot acquisition tracker
Create one-unit pilot cost model for 3 candidate lodging types
Create design token file and media manifest from approved photos
Stand up staging website shell with route stubs and CMS model
Set up Lodgify/Hospitable, Stripe, and email provider sandboxes
Build Burro internal assistant with property/artisan planning modules only
Define workshop SKUs, pricing ladders, and intake forms
Create activism content model and update feed

risk_register.title: Water access and sustainability failure
mitigation: Do not scale beyond the one-unit pilot until water plan is validated.
Model hauled water vs. well vs. rain capture explicitly.
Track usage by zone and publish water stewardship rules.

risk_register.title: Extreme seasonality causes weak summer economics
mitigation: Price and forecast by season, not by annual averages.
Bias summer programming toward night-first, workshop, and retreat products.
Avoid counting on daytime summer excursions as core revenue.

risk_register.title: Property title/probate delays block infrastructure or resale decisions
mitigation: Track liens, probate contacts, and appraisal anomalies in property tracker.
Separate build decisions requiring clear title from non-title-dependent planning tasks.

risk_register.title: Operator overload and feature fatigue
mitigation: Use build-now / activate-later model.
Default new features to R0 or R1 readiness.
Use templates and assistant-generated drafts instead of blank-slate workflows.

risk_register.title: Equipment damage or misuse destroys rental margins
mitigation: Start with one machine and a strong inspection/deposit process.
Bias toward local delivery and operator orientation.
Treat second-machine purchase as post-proof decision only.

risk_register.title: Border-wall policy changes degrade destination value
mitigation: Maintain activism hub and local coalition ties.
Use livestream/media content to document scenic and dark-sky value.
Frame conservation as core brand pillar.

risk_register.title: Heavy fragile artisan shipping produces losses and support burden
mitigation: Keep public ecommerce narrow.
Use local pickup and workshop follow-up for heavy products.
Offer freight or pickup-only for larger tile runs.

risk_register.title: Remote connectivity causes outages in guest-facing tools
mitigation: Use degraded offline-safe modes for Burro and site content.
Cache critical FAQs, maps, and check-in data locally.
Treat livestreaming as non-critical.

deferred_scope.item: Owned cross-border ferry or boat operation
deferred_scope.reason: regulatory complexity, citizenship, liability, cross-border permitting

enhancements.title: Payment Activation Preparation
enhancements.description: Stripe integration ready. Manual handoff active. Coming Soon badges displayed.

enhancements.title: Site Visual Overhaul
enhancements.description: Coming Soon badges, payment stubs, Steel Buildings page, Dark Sky/Rentals page conversions

publish_contract.intended_next_step: use as source pack for implementation planning, sprint design, and code generation
